response.justification: The candidate has roughly 19 years of hands-on software development experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, plus genuine entrepreneurial experience founding and sustaining an open-source geo-data platform. However, the role is a director-level IC position centered on AI strategy, and the resume shows only recent, light exposure to AI (MCP code generators and agentic tooling since 2025), no recognized AI expertise or shipped AI products at scale, no advanced degree in an AI-related field, and no evidence of organization-level technical leadership, strategic advisory work, or influencing product direction beyond team scope. Strong senior engineer, but not a fit for a principal AI-strategy mandate.
strengths: 19+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency distributed systems
Entrepreneurial track record: founded and maintains an open-source geo-data platform used in production by an NGO network
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, open-source libraries, workshops, and conference talks demonstrating knowledge sharing
concerns: Minimal demonstrated AI/ML depth — only ~1 year of AI-agent tooling work, no shipped AI products or recognized AI expertise
No evidence of director/principal-level scope: career history is senior IC delivery work, not strategic or cross-org technical leadership
Limited cloud-platform strategy experience — cloud usage is applied (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) rather than platform/product-defining

key_factors.factor: AI expertise gap vs. AI-strategy mandate
Seniority mismatch (senior IC vs. principal/director scope)
Long, broad engineering experience with entrepreneurial background
